agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation, trials) with payment validation, retry logic, and tier-specific grace periods.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, progressing through states: trial, active, grace period, cancelled."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect subscription fees. Has a status indicating whether it can successfully process charges."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring period (e.g., monthly, annual) that defines when a subscriber is charged."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The automated process of charging a subscriber's payment method to extend their subscription for another billing cycle."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A window of time after renewal failure during which a subscription remains active before cancellation is enforced."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A time-limited subscription that grants access without payment, limited to one active instance per email address."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription level that grants enhanced benefits, including a 14-day grace period instead of the standard 3-strike cancellation rule."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated with a failed payment method on file.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription against a known-bad payment method creates a corrupt state — the system would be providing a paid service with no ability to collect revenue. This is equivalent to shipping an order to an invalid address. No business policy change should allow deliberately entering a state where revenue collection is impossible from the start.","testable_assertion":"Given a payment method with status 'failed', any attempt to activate a subscription using that payment method MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy choice designed to give operational breathing room for retries. A VP could reasonably change this to 5 days, 10 days, or 3 days based on payment processor SLAs, retry success rate data, or customer feedback. The system is not corrupt if this window changes — it is a tunable parameter.","testable_assertion":"The renewal process MUST be initiated exactly 7 days before the current billing cycle's end date."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The threshold of 3 attempts is a business policy decision balancing customer retention against revenue risk. This number could be changed to 2 or 5 based on churn analysis, payment processor behavior, or competitive pressure. The system's data integrity is not compromised by changing this threshold — it is a configurable policy, not a physical or logical constraint.","testable_assertion":"When a non-premium subscription accumulates 3 consecutive failed renewal payment attempts, the system MUST transition the subscription to 'cancelled' status."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state transition integrity constraint. 'Cancelled' is a terminal state in the subscription lifecycle. Allowing reactivation of a cancelled subscription would corrupt the audit trail, potentially re-enable stale payment methods, bypass current pricing/terms, and create ambiguity about which billing cycle the customer is in. The business explicitly encodes that the cancelled-to-active transition does not exist. This is a hard constraint on the state machine, not a soft preference.","testable_assertion":"Given a subscription in 'cancelled' status, any attempt to transition it to 'active' status MUST be rejected. The only path to active service is creating a new subscription entity."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 14-day window and its applicability to the premium tier are business policy decisions. The company could change the grace period to 21 days, extend it to all tiers, or revert premium subscribers to the strike-based model. This is a retention strategy lever, not a data integrity constraint. No system state becomes corrupt if the duration or eligibility changes.","testable_assertion":"When a premium tier subscription's renewal payment fails, the subscription MUST remain active for 14 calendar days before being cancelled, and the 3-strike counter rule MUST NOT apply."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data consistency and abuse-prevention constraint. Allowing multiple concurrent active trials per email would represent a corrupt state — the business model of a trial is predicated on it being a one-time experience per identity. Unlike the retry count or grace period duration, this isn't a tunable threshold; it's a uniqueness constraint that protects the integrity of the trial concept itself. Violating it doesn't just change policy — it breaks the definition of what a trial is.","testable_assertion":"Given an email address with an existing subscription in 'trial' status, any attempt to create a second trial subscription for that same email MUST be rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer with a payment method that has status 'failed'","when":"The customer attempts to activate a subscription","then":"The system rejects the activation and returns an error indicating the payment method must be valid"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription with a billing cycle ending in 7 days","when":"The system runs the renewal scheduler","then":"A renewal charge is initiated against the subscriber's payment method"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A standard-tier subscription with 2 prior failed renewal attempts","when":"The third renewal attempt fails","then":"The subscription is automatically transitioned to 'cancelled' status"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription in 'cancelled' status","when":"Any actor attempts to reactivate or resume the subscription","then":"The system rejects the operation and directs the customer to create a new subscription"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A premium-tier subscription whose renewal payment has just failed","when":"The system evaluates cancellation rules","then":"The subscription enters a 14-day grace period and the 3-strike counter is not applied"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A premium-tier subscription that has been in grace period for 14 days without successful payment","when":"The grace period expires","then":"The subscription is automatically cancelled"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An email address that already has an active trial subscription","when":"A request is made to create another trial subscription for that email","then":"The system rejects the request with an error indicating only one active trial per email is allowed"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An email address whose previous trial subscription has ended (expired or converted)","when":"A request is made to create a new trial subscription for that email","then":"The system evaluates the request — note: the spec says 'at any time' (concurrent), not 'ever', so a new trial after the prior one ends may be permissible"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Payment method fails between subscription creation and activation","expected_behavior":"The activation check must validate payment method status at the moment of activation, not at creation time. A method valid at creation may have failed since."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber's payment succeeds on day 13 of the grace period","expected_behavior":"Grace period ends, subscription remains active, billing cycle resets. The 14-day window is a maximum, not a fixed duration."},{"scenario":"Renewal attempt fails on attempt 2, then the payment method is updated","expected_behavior":"Spec is ambiguous — does the counter reset on payment method update? This needs clarification. See escalations."},{"scenario":"Customer has a cancelled subscription and a new active subscription simultaneously","expected_behavior":"This is valid. The cancelled subscription remains as a historical record. The new subscription is an independent entity."},{"scenario":"Free trial ends and customer immediately requests another trial","expected_behavior":"The rule says 'at any time' — implying concurrency, not lifetime. Once the first trial is no longer active, creating a new trial may be allowed. This is ambiguous and needs product clarification."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber downgrades to standard tier during grace period","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — does the grace period survive the tier change or does the 3-strike rule immediately apply? Needs clarification."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"These rules directly protect revenue. BR-1 prevents revenue-less activations. BR-2/BR-3/BR-5 balance revenue recovery (retry window) against bad debt exposure. BR-6 prevents trial abuse that delays conversion to paid.","operational":"Auto-renewal 7 days early creates a retry window that reduces involuntary churn. The premium grace period requires tier-aware cancellation logic, adding operational complexity but improving retention for highest-value customers.","user":"The cancelled-cannot-reactivate rule is the most user-facing friction point — customers who accidentally let subscriptions lapse must re-onboard. Premium grace period rewards high-value customers with better treatment. Trial uniqueness prevents abuse but may frustrate legitimate re-trials.","risk":"Primary risk is in the ambiguities: retry counter reset behavior, trial lifetime vs concurrency interpretation, and tier-change during grace period. Incorrect implementation of any could either hemorrhage revenue or unjustly cancel paying customers.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (Standard Tier)","impact":"Subject to 3-strike cancellation with no grace period — faster involuntary churn if payment issues arise"},{"group":"Customers (Premium Tier)","impact":"Better retention protection via 14-day grace period — higher perceived value of premium tier"},{"group":"Finance/Revenue Team","impact":"Directly affected by renewal timing, retry logic, and trial abuse prevention — all rules have revenue implications"},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle escalations from cancelled subscribers who want reactivation and are told they must create new subscriptions"},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Must implement a state machine with terminal states, tier-aware cancellation logic, and email-scoped uniqueness constraints"}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec contains two significant ambiguities (retry counter reset behavior, trial 'at any time' meaning) and one implicit gap (tier changes during grace period). These create real implementation risk. The core rules are clear, but the edge cases where rules interact are underspecified.","high_confidence_areas":["BR-1: Payment validation at activation is unambiguous","BR-4: Cancelled as terminal state is clearly stated","BR-6: One active trial per email concurrency constraint is clear in the simple case"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether failed attempt counter resets when payment method is updated","Whether 'one active trial per email at any time' means concurrent or lifetime","Behavior when a subscriber changes tier during an active grace period","Whether premium grace period and 3-strike rule can ever overlap (e.g., downgrade scenario)"],"assumptions":["'Failed payment method' means a payment method with a known bad status, not one that has never been attempted","The 7-day renewal window applies to all tiers equally","Premium 14-day grace period completely replaces the 3-strike model rather than augmenting it","'At any time' in the trial rule means concurrent active trials, not lifetime trial count"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Does the failed renewal attempt counter reset when a customer updates their payment method? The spec says '3 failed renewal attempts' but does not specify whether updating the payment method resets this counter. This materially affects involuntary churn rates.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers (Standard Tier)","Finance/Revenue Team","Customer Support"],"options":["Counter resets on payment method update (customer-friendly, reduces involuntary churn)","Counter never resets within a billing cycle (revenue-protective, simpler to implement)","Counter resets only if new payment method is validated successfully (balanced approach)"],"recommendation":"Option C — reset counter only on successful payment method validation. This balances customer fairness with abuse prevention."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The trial rule states 'one active trial per email address at any time.' Does 'at any time' mean no concurrent active trials (allowing sequential trials after one ends), or does it mean lifetime — only one trial ever per email? This fundamentally changes the trial conversion funnel.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers (Trial)","Finance/Revenue Team","Growth/Marketing Team"],"options":["Concurrent only: a new trial is allowed after the previous one ends or converts","Lifetime: only one trial ever per email address, regardless of prior trial status"],"recommendation":"Concurrent only — the phrase 'active trial' implies the constraint is about simultaneous state, not history. But product team must confirm, as lifetime restriction is a common pattern for abuse prevention."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"What happens when a premium subscriber downgrades to standard tier during an active 14-day grace period? Does the grace period survive, or does the 3-strike rule immediately take effect?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers (Premium Tier)","Finance/Revenue Team","Engineering"],"options":["Grace period survives the downgrade (honor the terms under which the grace period started)","3-strike rule applies immediately upon downgrade","Grace period continues but with adjusted remaining time"],"recommendation":"Option A — honor the grace period that was granted under the premium terms. Retroactively changing terms mid-grace-period would be legally and ethically problematic."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
